summary: Vietnam's work is led by the Government Cipher Committee, the national cryptographic agency, which is building domestic post-quantum research capacity rather than publishing an adopted national standard. Its research bodies are developing quantum-resistant schemes, including hash-based digital signatures and domestic post-quantum signature work, as the country prepares for the quantum-computer era.
govActors: Ban Cơ yếu Chính phủ (Government Cipher Committee) | National cryptographic agency directing post-quantum research and advising on national quantum-resistant cryptography
provenance: summary + govActors: the two included results rows (both Government Cipher Committee journal articles) on hash-based quantum-resistant digital signature schemes (VN-PQSign) and continued in-depth quantum cryptography research

dominantProcess: OMITTED. The included material shows domestic research (hash-based signatures, VN-PQSign) and the broader candidates reference both NIST-lineage algorithms (Kyber/ML-KEM, Dilithium) and a domestic national standard, with no adopted national policy naming a single camp. Genuinely unclear, so left grey per the iron rule - REVIEW
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED. No FIPS family or specific algorithm is adopted in the included rows; CRYSTALS-Dilithium and Kyber appear only in included:false research items

migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED. The 2030-2035 era and a 2026-2030 plan appear only in included:false T1 articles and journalism in the candidates, not in the two included documents - REVIEW
hybridDeployment: OMITTED. A move to hybrid cryptography is described only in an included:false migration article

dominantProcess re-classified Sovereign by best-available signal (medium): the Government Cipher Committee is building its own national quantum-resistant schemes - hash-based VN-PQSign and nationally developed standards/HSMs - and is advising on national quantum-resistant standards, a genuine own-scheme effort rather than mere adoption (NIST-lineage Kyber/Dilithium appear only as research inputs). Provisional, flagged for review
Model 2026-06-14 (sovereign-bloc / sovereign-developer, Low): VN-PQSign is a genuine indigenous effort but at research/announcement stage, not a mandated national suite. Emerging.
